scope: Operational semantics for default/manual/dormant/candidate-prune migration of skills and package surfaces.

constraints: Tier migration must reuse enable/disable/list/audit/sync; no new migration control-plane is introduced.
remove/add are reserved for separately approved prune/restore workflows after candidate-prune review closure.
Dormant and candidate-prune package surfaces remain disabled/inactive and policy-documented.
Package tiering does not introduce a new package command surface.

skills.operationalDefinition: Registered and baseline-enabled capability used by first-pass routing for the planning->audit->debug default spine.

packages.operationalDefinition: Surface participates in baseline operational path without explicit specialist opt-in.
packages.activation: Baseline-on only when core-loop critical and low-noise.

skills.operationalDefinition: Discoverable, explicitly requestable capability that is not default-ranked in baseline routing.
skills.discoverability: Appears in list --all while remaining outside baseline default selection.
skills.activation: Explicit user request, explicit task context, or explicit profile/session overlay only.

requiredState.notes: Baseline disabled via skills-manage; temporary explicit activation can use enable then disable after use.

packages.operationalDefinition: Explicitly invoked package surface (CLI or MCP wrapper) that is not part of baseline default spine.

skills.operationalDefinition: Retained but inactive capability for contingency/migration safety; not eligible for routine default or routine manual invocation.
skills.activation: Requires reactivation decision with current evidence before enable.

packages.operationalDefinition: Retained surface intentionally inactive and tracked in dormant policy.

skills.operationalDefinition: Staged review state for low-value/redundant capability. Candidate-prune is recommendation posture, not deletion.
skills.discoverability: Kept in evidence inventory for review/rollback traceability.

packages.operationalDefinition: Surface retained in inactive state while queued for explicit prune review.

candidate-prune.hardRule: candidate-prune must never imply immediate deletion.

requiredEvidence: No core-loop blocker identified by current scenario taxonomy
skills-manage list/audit output shows safe disabled state
Owner approval and rollback metadata prepared
skillsThresholds.exceptionPath: Direct manual->candidate-prune allowed only with explicit emergency rationale and signed rollback plan.

requiredSignals: No default-tier dependency in cohesion map
Sustained inactive/dormant posture across review cycles
Weak or no scenario fit compared with existing alternatives
requiredEvidence: If MCP-related, dormant policy entry present and current
Owner confirmation that removal is optional and reversible until final prune approval

default_to_manual: Evidence of reduced routine core-loop necessity or increased misroute/noise
At least one recent audit showing manual posture is viable
Rollback commands and pre-change snapshots captured
manual_to_dormant: No active dependency chain requiring routine manual invocation
dormant_to_candidate_prune: No successful reactivation evidence in review window
Deletion explicitly deferred pending separate approval workflow

candidate_prune_to_dormant: Rollback plan for returning to candidate-prune if evidence weakens
dormant_to_manual: Concrete request history proving active specialist value
manual_to_default: Repeated core-loop dependency (not one-off specialist demand)
Low false-activation risk versus current default set
Audit and sync --dry-run checks clean after enable

packageActions: Remove from baseline/default profile mapping while keeping surface available for explicit invocation
Promote to baseline profile only after promotion evidence thresholds are met
Add or update dormant entry in opencode-config/mcp-dormant-policy.json when MCP-related
rollback: Keep disabled baseline unless explicit manual activation is requested

skillActions: Optionally node scripts/skills-manage.mjs enable <skill> only for explicit approved activation
packageActions: Update dormant policy reactivation status or remove entry when no longer dormant

skillActions: Record candidate-prune evidence and review deadline
packageActions: Keep surface inactive and retain dormant-policy trace

skillActions: Keep disabled until manual/default promotion evidence exists
packageActions: Maintain inactive wrapper with dormant posture

conditions: Must document why dormant staging is skipped
Must include explicit rollback commands and owner sign-off
rollback: If changed, restore prior enable/disable state from snapshot

conditions: Strong fresh evidence of sustained specialist demand
rollback: Return to candidate-prune metadata and disabled state

guardrails: Direct jumps to/from default are discouraged and require exception evidence + rollback metadata.
Every transition record must include rollback instructions before execution.
candidate-prune recommendation is non-destructive until separate prune approval is granted.
